heading: Definitions
label: 241 Definitions
notes: CodificationThis chapter constitutes part C of “An act making appropriations for the Department of Agriculture for the fiscal year ending , and for other purposes,” approved . Part A of , containing the “United States Cotton Futures Act” formerly classified to chapter 13 of Title 26, Internal Revenue Code, was repealed by section 4 of , . L. 106–472, title II, § 202114 Stat. 2068Nov. 9, 2000“(a).—Proposed RegulationsNot later than 90 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Secretary of Agriculture shall publish in the Federal Register proposed regulations for carrying out the amendment made by section 201 [enacting this chapter].Nov. 9, 2000“(b).—Final RegulationsNot later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall promulgate final regulations for carrying out the amendment made by section 201.“(c).—Effectiveness of Existing ActThe United States Warehouse Act ( et seq.) (as it existed before the amendment made by section 201) shall be effective until the earlier of—7 U.S.C. 241“(1)the date on which final regulations are promulgated under subsection (b); or“(2).”August 1, 2001

children.heading: Agricultural product
children.label: 1 Agricultural product
children.text: The term “agricultural product” means an agricultural commodity, as determined by the Secretary, including a processed product of an agricultural commodity.

children.heading: Approval
children.label: 2 Approval
children.text: The term “approval” means the consent provided by the Secretary for a person to engage in an activity authorized by this chapter.

children.heading: Department
children.label: 3 Department
children.text: The term “Department” means the Department of Agriculture.

children.heading: Electronic document
children.label: 4 Electronic document
children.text: The term “electronic document” means a document that is generated, sent, received, or stored by electronic, optical, or similar means, including electronic data interchange, electronic mail, telegram, telex, or telecopy.

children.heading: Electronic receipt
children.label: 5 Electronic receipt
children.text: The term “electronic receipt” means a receipt that is authorized by the Secretary to be issued or transmitted under this chapter in the form of an electronic document.

children.heading: Holder
children.label: 6 Holder
children.text: The term “holder” means a person that has possession in fact or by operation of law of a receipt or any electronic document.

children.label: A
children.text: a person (as defined in );section 1 of title 1

children.label: C
children.text: a political subdivision of a State.

children.heading: Receipt
children.label: 8 Receipt
children.text: The term “receipt” means a warehouse receipt issued in accordance with this chapter, including an electronic receipt.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 9 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: Warehouse
children.label: 10 Warehouse
children.text: The term “warehouse” means a structure or other approved storage facility, as determined by the Secretary, in which any agricultural product may be stored or handled for the purposes of interstate or foreign commerce.

children.heading: Warehouse operator
children.label: 11 Warehouse operator
children.text: The term “warehouse operator” means a person that is lawfully engaged in the business of storing or handling agricultural products.
